gate.title: Get more from ToolBoxy
gate.desc: Create a free account to save your history, bookmark your favorite tools, and get a personal dashboard.
gate.signin: Already have an account? Sign in

spinner-wheel.intro: Enter options and spin the wheel to pick one at random.

faq.q: How many options can I add?
faq.a: There's no hard limit, but with too many the labels get small, so around 12 reads best.
No. It's chosen at random in your browser each spin, and every option has an equal chance.

ladder-game.intro: Enter names and results and follow the ladder to match them at random.

faq.q: Can the names and results counts differ?
faq.a: No, they must be equal. If the counts differ, the ladder won't be shown.
No. The ladder is generated at random each time, and you can rebuild it with 'Reshuffle ladder'.

text-to-speech.intro: Type text and it reads it aloud. Choose a voice and adjust speed, pitch, and volume.

faq.q: No sound / the voice list is empty.
faq.a: It uses the text-to-speech voices installed on your browser/device. If the list is empty, add a voice in your system settings. Some mobile browsers only play sound after you tap the screen once.

faq.q: Can I save the spoken audio as a file?
faq.a: No. Browser speech synthesis only supports live playback, not exporting an audio file. Everything runs on your device and your text is never sent anywhere.

daedarani.intro: Follow the Great Compassion Dharani by its reading and chant it with a 7/21/108 repetition counter.

faq.a: The Great Compassion Dharani is a transliterated mantra, traditionally chanted for its sound rather than translated. So it shows only the reading (and the Japanese 大悲呪 reading), with no Hanja or meaning.
Press ▶ and each full pass of the dharani adds one automatically, stopping at your goal (7, 21, 108). When chanting yourself, use +1 to count and ↺ to reset. The count is saved in this browser.

banyasimgyeong.intro: Read the Heart Sutra with the Hanja original, reading, and meaning together, and follow along with spoken recitation.

faq.a: The original and reading follow the standard recitation; the meaning is ToolBoxy's own plain rendering, so its wording differs from any standard translation.
Your device needs a Korean text-to-speech voice installed. Without one, the line highlight and auto-scroll still run, just without sound.

cheonsugyeong.intro: Read the Cheonsugyeong with the Hanja original, Korean reading, and meaning together, and follow along with spoken recitation.

faq.a: The original Hanja and reading are public domain; the meaning is ToolBoxy's own plain rendering, so its wording differs from any standard translation.
Your device needs a Korean text-to-speech voice installed. Without one, the line highlight and auto-scroll still run, just without sound.

white-noise-machine.intro: Play soothing background sounds — rain, fire, ocean, and more — to help you focus, sleep, or relax, all generated right in your browser.

faq.a: Rain, a crackling fire, ocean waves, a fan, pencil writing, and pure white, pink, and brown noise.
Yes. The sounds are generated in your browser with the Web Audio API, so once the page has loaded it keeps playing without an internet connection.

romaji-kana-converter.intro: Type romaji (Latin letters) and instantly get hiragana or katakana, or paste kana to get romaji back. Everything runs in your browser.

faq.a: Yes — completely free with no account or installation required.

faq.q: Does it handle long words and small tsu?
faq.a: Yes. Double consonants become っ (sokuon) and youon like kya/sha are supported.

fullwidth-halfwidth-converter.intro: Paste text to convert between full-width (全角) and half-width (半角) ASCII letters, digits, symbols and spaces.

faq.a: Yes, free and runs entirely in your browser.
ASCII letters, numbers, punctuation and spaces. Kanji and kana are left unchanged.

hiragana-katakana-converter.intro: Convert hiragana to katakana or katakana to hiragana instantly. Useful for Japanese study and proofreading.

faq.a: Yes, free with no sign-up.
Kana mapping is one-to-one, so conversion is exact.

hangul-romaja-converter.intro: Convert Korean Hangul into Latin romanization using the Revised Romanization system. Great for names, places and study.

faq.a: Yes, free and browser-based.

batch-image-converter.intro: Add images, set your options, and process them all at once — right in your browser.

faq.a: There's no fixed limit — it runs in your browser, so it depends on your device's memory. Dozens to a few hundred images work smoothly; very large batches may take a little longer.
At high quality the difference is hard to notice. PNG output is lossless; JPEG and WebP use the quality you choose.

batch-image-compressor.intro: Add images, set your options, and process them all at once — right in your browser.

faq.q: How many images can I process at once?
faq.a: There's no fixed limit — it runs in your browser, so it depends on your device's memory. Dozens to a few hundred images work smoothly; very large batches may take a little longer.
Compression trades some quality for smaller files — that's the point — but you control the quality level, and the preview shows the size saved.

batch-image-watermark.intro: Add images, set your options, and process them all at once — right in your browser.

faq.q: How many images can I process at once?
faq.a: There's no fixed limit — it runs in your browser, so it depends on your device's memory. Dozens to a few hundred images work smoothly; very large batches may take a little longer.
Only the watermark is added on top — the rest of the image is re-encoded at high quality, keeping the original size.

batch-image-rename.intro: Add images, set your options, and process them all at once — right in your browser.

faq.q: How many images can I process at once?
faq.a: There's no fixed limit — it runs in your browser, so it depends on your device's memory. Dozens to a few hundred images work smoothly; very large batches may take a little longer.
No — the image data is untouched. Only the filename changes, and the renamed copies are packed into a zip (browsers can't rename your originals in place).

batch-image-editor.intro: Upload once, turn on the tasks you want, and apply them together.

faq.q: Can I run several tasks at once?
faq.a: Yes — turn on any combination and they apply top to bottom in one pass. Rename is always applied last (it changes the filename, the others change the image).

faq.q: How many images can I process at once?
faq.a: There's no fixed limit — it runs in your browser, so it depends on your device's memory. Dozens to a few hundred work smoothly; very large batches with many tasks may take longer.
Resize/convert/compress re-encode at the quality you choose (PNG is lossless). Each enabled task re-encodes once, so chaining many lossy steps can add up — use only what you need.

screen-capture.intro: Pick a screen to share, capture it as one image, then save or copy it.

faq.q: What happens when I click Capture?
faq.a: Your browser opens a picker to choose the screen, window, or tab to share. The selected screen is captured as a single image and sharing ends immediately.

faq.q: Does it work on a phone?
faq.a: No. Screen capture (getDisplayMedia) only works in Chrome, Edge, or Firefox on desktop. Mobile browsers are not supported.

faqCommon.a: Yes — it is completely free to use, with no account or sign-up required.
faqCommon.aboutTail: You can use it for free with no sign-up or installation — just open the page and start right away.
faqCommon.uploadPrivacyQ: Are my uploaded files sent to a server?
faqCommon.uploadPrivacyA: No. Everything runs only on your own computer, inside your browser, and your files are never sent to a server or anywhere else. So you can use it with complete peace of mind, without worrying about your file contents leaking.

toolAbout.todo-list: A simple checklist for jotting tasks and ticking them off one by one. Edit or delete items and drag to reorder them. Your list is saved automatically in this browser only, so it stays even after you close and reopen the window.
toolAbout.resistor-color-code: Read a resistor's value from its color bands. Choose 4 or 5 bands, pick each band's color, and it computes the resistance (Ω/kΩ/MΩ/GΩ) and tolerance (±%) while previewing the bands on a resistor graphic — handy for quickly checking color codes when working with electronics. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.blood-type-inheritance: Find the blood types a child can have from the parents' blood types. Based on ABO inheritance (A and B are dominant, O recessive), pick both parents' types and it shows which child blood types are possible and which are impossible at a glance. Actual probabilities depend on the parents' genotypes (AA/AO, etc.), so this shows whether each type is possible. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.scientific-notation-converter: Convert very large or small numbers between scientific notation and plain decimals. Type something like 1500000 or 1.5e6 or 1.5×10^6 and it shows the scientific notation (a×10ⁿ), E-notation (1.5e6), and the plain decimal together. Handy for homework and lab data, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.planet-weight-calculator: Enter your weight on Earth and this tool shows what you'd weigh on the Sun, Mercury, Venus, the Moon, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto. It multiplies by each body's surface gravity (Earth = 1) — for example you'd weigh about 1/6 as much on the Moon and about 2.5× as much on Jupiter. Only your weight changes with gravity; your mass stays the same. Fun to share, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.age-on-other-planets: Enter your birthdate and this tool converts your age into years on Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, and the other planets. Each planet takes a different time to orbit the Sun (its year), so the same lifespan is a very different age elsewhere (a year on Mars is about 687 days, so your Mars age is roughly half your Earth age). It also shows the number of days you've lived, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.ocr: Recognize the text inside a photo or screenshot (OCR) and turn it into editable, copyable text. It supports Korean, English, and Japanese (plus Korean+English), and once recognition finishes you can edit or copy the result directly. The first run downloads language data and takes a moment, and clear, straight images give the best accuracy. Everything is processed in your browser, so your image is never uploaded, and it's free with no install.
toolAbout.video-to-gif: Cut a clip from a video and turn it into an animated GIF. Adjust the start point, length, width, and FPS (frames per second) to balance file size and smoothness. It works by capturing the video's frames and encoding them into a GIF, and the whole process runs in your browser, so your video is never uploaded. Free with no install.
toolAbout.image-filter: Adjust a photo's brightness, contrast, saturation, and hue with sliders, and apply one-tap presets like black & white, sepia, vintage, sharpen, warm, and cool. Tweak the values against a live preview and save the result as PNG; you can also add blur. Everything is processed in your browser, so your photo is never uploaded to a server, and it's free with no install.
toolAbout.man-age-calculator: Calculate your Korean 'man-nai' (full international age) instantly from your birthdate. Korea standardized legal and official ages to man-nai in June 2023; this tool shows that age along with the traditional counting age (this year − birth year + 1) and year age (this year − birth year), plus the days until your next birthday. Everything is processed in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.dday-calculator: Count how many days remain until a target date — an exam, anniversary, or deadline. Enter the date and it shows the days left as D-100, D-1, and so on, or D+number for a date that has already passed. You can add a name to label what the countdown is for. It's calculated from today, runs in your browser, and is free with no install.
toolAbout.world-clock: See the current time in major cities like Seoul, Tokyo, New York, London, and Sydney on one screen, updating in real time. Handy for checking the time difference when you have family or colleagues abroad; cities on a different calendar day are shown with their date. It updates every second based on your device clock, free with no install.
toolAbout.online-alarm: Set a time and this online alarm alerts you with a sound when it arrives. Add a note to label what the alarm is for, and once set you'll see a live countdown to the alarm. The sound is generated in your browser, and the tab needs to stay open for the alarm to ring. Free with no install.
toolAbout.refresh-rate-test: Measures your screen's frames per second (FPS) in real time with requestAnimationFrame to estimate the monitor's refresh rate. Browsers usually render in sync with the refresh rate, so the reading lands close to real values like 60, 120, 144, or 165Hz. It also shows your screen resolution, browser viewport size, pixel ratio (DPR), and color depth. Everything is measured in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.screen-test: Fills the whole screen with solid colors — red, green, blue, white, black, and more — to check your monitor for dead pixels and burn-in. Tap a color to go fullscreen, then click (or tap) to cycle to the next color. A dot that never changes color on a solid background is a dead pixel, and the black and white screens help check OLED burn-in. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.speaker-test: Plays sound on the left, right, or both channels so you can verify your speakers or earphones output correctly. Quickly check whether your earphones are swapped left-to-right or whether one side is silent. The sound is generated in your browser (a 440Hz sine wave), free with no install.
toolAbout.mic-test: Shows the loudness of the sound coming into your microphone as a live bar. Before a video call or recording, check that the mic is on and picking up sound clearly. Audio never leaves your device — it's used only for the level meter and is never recorded or saved. Just allow permission and it works in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.keyboard-test: Press a key and it lights up on the on-screen keyboard. Keys you've pressed turn green, so you can instantly spot a stuck or dead key that doesn't respond. It also shows the name and code of the last key pressed. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.anniversary-calculator: Enter a start date — the day you met, a wedding anniversary, anything — and this tool shows how many days it's been as of today, plus the dates and countdown (D-day) for key milestones like 100, 200, and 1000 days and 1, 2, and more years. The start date counts as day 1 (Korean style), so day 100 is 99 days after the start. Everything is calculated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.chinese-zodiac: Enter a year to see its Chinese zodiac animal (Rat, Ox, Tiger…), its sexagenary cycle name (ganzhi, e.g. 甲辰), and its element and color. For example, 2024 is 甲辰, the 'Blue Dragon.' It also lists other years with the same animal. It's based on the Gregorian year (births before Lunar New Year may belong to the previous animal), and everything is calculated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.country-calling-codes: Search for a country and this tool gives its international calling code (e.g. South Korea +82, United States +1). Search by country name (Korean, English, or Japanese) or by number, and tap a code to copy it. It's the code you add before a number when calling internationally. It covers major countries, runs entirely in your browser, and is free with no install.
toolAbout.flag-quiz: Shows flags from countries around the world and asks you to pick the right country from four choices. Your correct answers and total are tracked as a score, so you can learn world flags the fun way. It runs entirely in your browser and is free with no install.
toolAbout.capital-quiz: Shows a country (its flag and name) and asks you to pick its capital from four choices. Great for studying world geography and capitals, with a score to track how you're doing. It runs entirely in your browser and is free with no install.
toolAbout.country-compare: Pick two countries and compare their capital, region, calling code, and population side by side. Handy for travel, study, or just curiosity when you want to size up countries quickly. Population figures are approximate, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.cheonjamun: Study the 1,000 characters of the Thousand Character Classic (천자문), grouped into 250 four-character verses you go through in order. Each character shows its Korean gloss and sound (e.g. 하늘 천 for 天), and tapping a character opens a tracing mode where you write over a faint guide. Your progress is saved automatically so you can pick up where you left off. The text and readings are standard, everything runs in your browser, and it's free with no install.
toolAbout.sajasohak: Learn the four-character verses of Sajasohak (사자소학), a classic Korean primer, organized by topic such as filial piety and friendship, with the hanja, reading, and meaning together. Move through the verses in order with your progress saved automatically. Great for an introduction to classical Chinese and character education. The text and readings are standard and the meanings are plain renderings; everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.gosaseongeo: Look up Sino-Korean four-character idioms (gosaseongeo) like 刻舟求劍 and 塞翁之馬 by their characters, Korean reading, initial consonants, or meaning. Each idiom has its literal and figurative meaning, origin story, source, an example sentence, and synonyms, all cross-linked to related idioms. You can also filter by character count (2–4) or 'has origin.' The origin notes and examples are written from scratch, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.idiom-quiz: A quiz game that shows the meaning of a Sino-Korean idiom and asks you to pick the right four-character idiom from four choices. Questions are drawn from the idiom dictionary, and your correct answers build a score — great for studying hanja and exam prep. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.game-2048: A number puzzle where you slide tiles in one direction to merge equal numbers and work toward the 2048 tile. Move with the arrow keys or by swiping; your score and best score are saved. The game ends when no moves remain. A quick, casual play, free with no install.
toolAbout.memory-game: A memory game where you flip cards to find the two matching pictures and clear all the pairs. Choose the number of cards (12, 16, or 20), and your moves and time are tracked. Fun for kids and adults alike, it runs entirely in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.tic-tac-toe: The classic game where you and your opponent take turns placing X and O on a 3×3 grid, and the first to make a row, column, or diagonal wins. Play against a flawless AI or with a friend in two-player mode. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.number-order: A focus game where you tap the numbers 1 to 25, scattered across a 5×5 grid, in order as fast as you can while it times you. Known as a Schulte table, it's used to train visual perception and concentration. Your best time is saved, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.color-find: A game where you find the one tile whose color is slightly different from the rest. A correct pick adds time and raises the level; higher levels add more tiles and shrink the color difference, so it gets harder. Good for training perception and focus, it runs entirely in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.aim-trainer: Click as many targets as you can in 30 seconds while it counts your hits. The target jumps to a new spot each time you click, so it's great for practicing mouse accuracy and reaction speed. Your best score is saved, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.sliding-puzzle: The classic puzzle where you slide numbered tiles 1 to 15 into the empty space on a 4×4 board to put them in order. It's shuffled into an always-solvable layout, and your move count is tracked. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.choseong-quiz: A Korean quiz that shows only a word's initial consonants (choseong — e.g. 감사 → ㄱㅅ) and asks you to guess the word. A category hint (food, animal, country…) helps you reason it out, and your correct answers build a score. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.minesweeper: The classic puzzle where you clear every safe square while avoiding hidden mines. Revealing a square shows the number of mines around it, and you use those clues to deduce where the mines are. Mark mines with flag mode (or right-click), and choose Easy, Medium, or Hard. Your first click is always safe, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.sudoku: Fill a 9×9 grid so each row, column, and 3×3 box contains 1 to 9 with no repeats. A fresh puzzle is generated each time, and only puzzles with a single unique solution are served. Choose a difficulty, and duplicate numbers in a row or column are highlighted in red. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.word-guess: A deduction game where you guess a hidden word within six tries. Korean mode compares two-syllable words by their jamo (the 'kkoturl' style), and English mode is the five-letter 'Wordle' style. Each guess gives color hints — green (right spot), yellow (in the word, wrong spot), and gray (not in it). Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.time-difference: Pick two countries — say Korea and the US — and this tool shows the time difference between them and each country's current time in real time. Handy for travel, business trips, or scheduling international calls and video meetings. It uses each country's capital time zone and applies daylight saving (DST) automatically, and everything is calculated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.plug-type: Pick the country you're traveling to and this tool shows its power outlet (plug) shape along with the mains voltage and frequency. Relative to Korea (Type C·F, 220V), it also tells you whether you'll need a travel adapter or to watch the voltage. Laptops and chargers usually accept 100–240V, so always check your device's input voltage. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.youtube-thumbnail: Paste a YouTube video URL (or video ID) and this tool shows the video's thumbnail in several resolutions — max, high, standard, and medium — ready to download. It recognizes short (youtu.be), regular, and Shorts links. Some videos don't have a max-resolution thumbnail, and thumbnails are copyrighted images, so use them for personal purposes only. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.savings-calculator: Enter your monthly deposit, term, and annual interest rate, and this tool calculates the amount you'll receive when an installment-savings account matures. Choose simple interest (the standard method) or monthly compounding, and taxed (15.4%) or tax-free to see the after-tax interest and final amount. It shows the principal, pre-tax interest, tax, and maturity amount at a glance. It's a reference estimate that may differ from an actual product, and everything is calculated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.social-character-counter: Type or paste your text and this tool counts characters (with and without spaces), words, and bytes, and shows how it fits each platform's character limit — X (Twitter), Instagram, Threads, Facebook, YouTube, TikTok, LinkedIn, and more — as remaining or over. Quickly check whether a post or caption fits. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.instagram-line-break: Instagram strips blank lines and line breaks from captions and bios, but this tool fills blank lines with an invisible character so your line breaks are kept. Write your text, press 'Convert & copy,' and paste it into Instagram to post with clean, separated lines. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.hashtag-generator: Enter keywords separated by commas, line breaks, or spaces, and this tool turns each into a hashtag (#), removes duplicates, and lets you copy them all at once. It also counts the hashtags and flags when you go over Instagram's recommended limit of 30. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.capital-distance: Pick two countries and this tool calculates the straight-line (great-circle) distance between their capitals in kilometers and miles. It uses the haversine formula to account for the Earth's curvature and find the shortest straight-line distance. Actual flight or travel distance may differ, and everything is calculated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.week-number-calculator: Enter a date and this tool tells you which week of the year it falls in, using the ISO 8601 standard. Along with the week number it shows the weekday, the Monday–Sunday range of that week, and the day of the year. Handy for quickly checking 'what week is it' for work and scheduling. Everything is calculated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.mouse-test: Checks your mouse's left, wheel, and right buttons, double-click, scroll direction, and polling rate (how often it reports its position). Move quickly inside the pad to see the reports per second (Hz) and gauge a gaming mouse's polling rate. It's also handy for spotting buttons that don't click or that chatter. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.metronome: An online metronome that plays a precise beat at the tempo you choose. Adjust from 40 to 240 BPM with the slider or ± buttons, pick the number of beats per measure (2/4 to 8/4), and accent the first beat. The on-screen dots blink in time so you can follow along visually. All sound is generated in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.zip-files: Bundle several files into a single ZIP archive. Drag and drop or pick files to add them to the list, then press Create ZIP to download one archive.zip. Files with the same name are automatically numbered so they don't clash. All zipping happens in your browser, so your files are never uploaded, and it's free with no install.
toolAbout.unzip: Open a ZIP archive and pull out the files inside. Upload a ZIP and it lists the files and their sizes, so you can download them one by one or all at once. All extraction happens in your browser, so your files are never uploaded. Password-protected ZIPs aren't supported, and it's free with no install.
toolAbout.compress-pdf: Reduce the file size of a PDF. It rasterizes each page and re-compresses it as an image, so it shrinks scanned documents and image-heavy PDFs the most (choose Strong, Balanced, or High quality). Note that text becomes part of the image, so it won't be selectable, copyable, or searchable. Everything runs in your browser, so your file is never uploaded to a server, and it's free with no install.
toolAbout.moon-phase-calculator: See the shape of the Moon (its phase) for any date, shown as a graphic. Pick a date and it tells you which of the eight phases it is — new, waxing crescent, first quarter, full, last quarter, and so on — along with the illumination and the moon's age in days. It's an approximation based on the synodic month (~29.53 days), so it may differ slightly from the real phase. Everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.ohms-law-calculator: Compute voltage, current, resistance, and power with Ohm's law. Enter any two of V (voltage), I (current), R (resistance), and P (power) and it works out the other two using V=IR and P=VI. Useful for electronics and physics work, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.element-quiz: Memorize the periodic table's element symbols and names with a 4-choice quiz. Pick a direction — symbol→name (S→Sulfur) or name→symbol (Sulfur→S) — and a range of 1–20 (basics), 1–54, or 1–118 (all). Choose an answer and it tells you right or wrong immediately while your score adds up. Element names appear in the current language (English/Korean/Japanese), and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.molar-mass-calculator: Enter a chemical formula and this tool computes its molar mass (molecular weight, g/mol). Type something like H2SO4 or C6H12O6 and it shows the total along with each element's count, atomic mass, and subtotal in a table. Parentheses (Ca(OH)2) and hydrate dots (CuSO4·5H2O) are recognized, and atomic masses are the standard atomic weights from the periodic table. Element symbols are case-sensitive (Na, Cl), and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.periodic-table: An interactive periodic table of all 118 chemical elements. Tap an element and a card shows its atomic number, mass, group, period, and category, with category colors and a legend to tell metals, nonmetals, halogens, noble gases, and more apart. Element names are shown in English, Korean, and Japanese (switch the site language and the card name follows); lanthanides and actinides sit in the two rows below the main table. Atomic masses are standard atomic weights, and everything runs in your browser, free with no install.
toolAbout.spinner-wheel: A spinner wheel that picks one option at random when you spin it. Use it to choose what's for lunch, decide who does the chores, or run a quick raffle — enter as many options as you like, one per line. Turn on 'Remove winner' and each pick is dropped from the wheel, which is handy for drawing several winners in a row. Everything runs in your browser, so it's free with no install.
toolAbout.ladder-game: A ladder game (amidakuji / ghost leg) that builds a random ladder to decide who gets what. Enter names and results, tap a number on top, and the path is drawn down the ladder with an animation to reveal the match. Reshuffle to build a new ladder, and keep the names and results the same count. Great for forfeits, assigning roles or chores, and seating — free with no install.
toolAbout.text-to-speech: A text-to-speech (TTS) tool that reads your text aloud. It uses your browser's built-in speech synthesis: paste your text, press ▶, and it speaks immediately. Choose from the voices installed on your device (different languages and genders), adjust the speed, pitch, and volume, and the word being spoken is highlighted as it reads. Everything runs on your device, so your text is never sent anywhere, and it's free with no install. Available voices and languages depend on your device and browser, and because it's a live playback tool, there's no option to save the audio as a file.
toolAbout.daedarani: The Great Compassion Dharani (신묘장구대다라니 / 大悲呪) lifted out of the Cheonsugyeong as a focused tool for daily repeated chanting. It is a transliterated mantra invoking the great compassion of Avalokiteśvara (Gwaneum), so it is chanted for its sound rather than translated. Press ▶ and it recites line by line, highlighting the current line and auto-scrolling; each full pass adds one to the count and it stops when you reach your goal (7, 21, or 108). Tap +1 to count rounds you chant yourself, and adjust speed, text size, and dark mode. In Japanese it is recited with the Japanese 大悲呪 reading. Everything runs in your browser, so it works offline with no installation.
toolAbout.banyasimgyeong: A viewer for reading and reciting the Heart Sutra (Banya-simgyeong / 般若心経). Toggle the Hanja original, the reading, and the meaning on or off and read them side by side; press Recite and it reads the sutra aloud one line at a time, highlighting the current line and auto-scrolling. Adjust speed, text size, loop, and dark mode, and save favorite lines with ★. The meaning is ToolBoxy's own plain rendering. Everything runs in your browser, so it works offline with no installation.
toolAbout.cheonsugyeong: A viewer for reading and reciting the Cheonsugyeong (Thousand-Hands Sutra). Toggle the Hanja original, the Korean reading, and the meaning on or off and read them side by side; press Recite and it reads the sutra aloud one line at a time, highlighting the current line and auto-scrolling so it's easy to follow. Lines that are chanted several times show a repeat count, and you can adjust the recitation speed. The original Hanja and reading are public domain; the meaning is ToolBoxy's own plain rendering, so its wording differs from any standard translation, and mantras (진언/다라니) are kept as transliterations rather than translated. Everything runs in your browser, so it works offline with no installation.
toolAbout.white-noise-machine: This white noise machine plays soothing background sounds to help you focus, sleep, or relax. Choose from rain, a crackling fire, ocean waves, a fan, a pencil writing, or pure white, pink, and brown noise, then adjust the volume and watch the elapsed-time counter. Every sound is generated live in your browser with the Web Audio API — there are no audio files to download, it keeps working offline once the page has loaded, and nothing is ever sent to a server.
toolAbout.pdf-to-jpg: This tool turns each page of a PDF into its own JPG image. Just upload a PDF and it renders every page as a picture you can preview and download one by one. It's handy when you want to share a document on social media as images, or keep only a few pages as photos. Everything happens right inside your browser, so your file is never uploaded to a server and stays completely private.
toolAbout.merge-pdf: This tool combines several PDF files into a single, tidy document. Add the files in the order you want and download them merged into one PDF. It's great for pulling together contracts, receipts, or reports that arrived as separate files. Since everything is processed inside your browser, nothing is uploaded — so even sensitive documents stay private.
toolAbout.qr-generator: Type in any link or text and this tool instantly creates a QR code for it. You can download the code as an image and place it on business cards, posters, menus, or product packaging — anywhere. It's perfect for sharing a website address or bridging printed materials to your online content. There's no app to install: you can make one in just a few seconds, right in your browser.
toolAbout.qr-scanner: Upload a photo or screenshot containing a QR code and this tool reads the link or text inside it right away. Even when a camera struggles to scan, you can just drop in a saved image to see what it contains. It's a safe way to check where a QR code leads before you actually visit it. The image is analyzed entirely in your browser and never uploaded, so your privacy stays protected.
toolAbout.image-compress: This tool shrinks the file size of your photos and images in a smart way. It keeps the quality as high as possible while making the file lighter, so uploads, email attachments, and messages go through much faster. It's especially useful if you post lots of photos to a blog or online store and want quicker page loads. Compression happens right in your browser, so your original photos never leave your device.
toolAbout.image-resizer: This tool changes the width and height of an image to whatever you need. Whether it's a profile picture, a thumbnail, or a social-media size requirement, just type in the dimensions and it resizes cleanly. You can scale it down while keeping the aspect ratio, or match an exact pixel size with ease. Everything runs in your browser, so it's fast and your original image is never sent to a server.
toolAbout.heic-to-jpg: This tool converts HEIC/HEIF photos taken on an iPhone into JPG files that open anywhere. HEIC is Apple's default format and often won't open on Windows PCs or in some apps and sites, but converting it clears up those compatibility headaches. Just upload a photo, press convert, and save it as a JPG. The conversion happens entirely in your browser, so your precious photos are never uploaded.
toolAbout.meme-generator: This tool lets you slap bold text onto an image to make a fun meme in seconds. Upload any picture, type your top and bottom captions, and it automatically adds the classic black-outlined meme lettering. It's perfect for whipping up a joke to send a friend or a post for social media. You can download the finished image right away, and since it all runs in your browser, there's nothing to upload.
toolAbout.bmi-calculator: Enter your height and weight, and this tool calculates your Body Mass Index (BMI) to show your health status at a glance. Along with the number, it shows which range you fall into — underweight, normal, or overweight — so you can easily see where you stand. It's a great starting point for setting a diet goal or managing your health. Your details aren't stored and are calculated right in your browser, so feel free to use it without worry.
toolAbout.password-generator: This tool instantly creates strong passwords that are hard to guess. Choose the length and whether to include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols, and it generates a random, secure password for you. It's a lifesaver when you want a different password for every account but can't keep dreaming them up. Everything is generated inside your browser and never stored or sent anywhere, so you can copy it with confidence.
toolAbout.word-counter: This tool counts the characters and words in your text in real time. Just type or paste your text and it instantly shows character counts (with and without spaces), word count, and even sentence count. It's especially reassuring when you're writing something with a length limit, like a cover letter, an assignment, or a social post. Your text is never stored anywhere and is processed entirely in your browser, so you can use it with peace of mind.
toolAbout.hangul-romaja-converter: This tool converts Korean (Hangul) names and words into their Roman-alphabet spelling. It follows the official Revised Romanization rules, so it's handy for writing the English version of your name on a passport, business card, or overseas paperwork. Type in Hangul and you get the romanized result instantly — no need to memorize every rule yourself. All conversion happens in your browser, so it's fast and the names you enter are never sent anywhere.
toolAbout.age-calculator: Enter your date of birth and this tool works out exactly how old you are in years, months, and days. It shows not just your age, but how many days you've been alive and how long until your next birthday — all at once. It's handy for filling out forms that need your exact age, or for keeping track of milestones. The date you enter isn't stored and is calculated instantly in your browser, so feel free to give it a try.
toolAbout.split-pdf: This tool splits one PDF into separate files. You can pull out specific pages or break a long document into smaller pieces, then download each part on its own. It's handy when you only need a few pages from a big report, or want to send someone just one section. Everything is processed in your browser, so the original file never leaves your device.
toolAbout.rotate-pdf: This tool rotates the pages of a PDF so they face the right way. If a scanned document came out sideways or upside down, you can turn the pages 90, 180, or 270 degrees and save a corrected copy. It's a quick fix for documents that are hard to read on screen or print awkwardly. The file is handled entirely in your browser and never uploaded.
toolAbout.remove-pdf-pages: This tool removes the pages you don't need from a PDF. Just pick the pages to delete and download a cleaner version without them. It's useful for taking out blank pages, cover sheets, or anything you'd rather not share. The whole process runs in your browser, so your document stays private.
toolAbout.pdf-to-text: This tool pulls the text out of a PDF so you can copy, edit, or reuse it. Upload a document and it extracts the words into plain text you can paste anywhere. It saves you from retyping content that's locked inside a PDF. Since it all happens in your browser, your file is never sent to a server.
toolAbout.image-to-pdf: This tool turns your images into a single PDF document. Add one or several pictures and they're combined into a tidy PDF you can download. It's perfect for submitting photos of receipts, IDs, or handwritten notes as one neat file. Everything is done in your browser, so your images are never uploaded.
toolAbout.watermark-pdf: This tool stamps a text watermark across every page of a PDF. Type a word like “Confidential” or “Draft” and it's added diagonally over each page before you download. It's a simple way to mark ownership or status on documents you share. The file is processed entirely in your browser and never uploaded.
toolAbout.pdf-page-numbers: This tool adds page numbers to a PDF in just a few clicks. Choose where the numbers should sit — bottom center, bottom right, or top right — and it labels every page for you. It's great for reports, contracts, or any document that should be easy to reference. Everything runs in your browser, so the file stays on your device.
toolAbout.image-crop: This tool lets you crop an image to keep just the part you want. Drag to select an area and download the trimmed result in seconds. It's handy for removing distractions, focusing on a subject, or fitting a photo to a specific shape. Cropping happens in your browser, so your original image is never uploaded.
toolAbout.rotate-image: This tool rotates and flips your images however you need. Turn a photo left or right, or mirror it horizontally and vertically, then download the corrected version. It's perfect for fixing a sideways photo or creating a mirrored effect. Everything is processed in your browser, so the image stays private and the quality is preserved.
toolAbout.circle-crop: This tool crops an image into a perfect circle with transparent corners. Upload a picture and it instantly becomes a round PNG, ideal for profile photos and avatars. No design software is needed — just upload and download. It all runs in your browser, so your image is never sent anywhere.
toolAbout.blur-image: This tool blurs an image, either as an effect or to hide sensitive details. Use the slider to set how strong the blur is, then download the result. It's handy for covering faces, names, or anything private before sharing a screenshot. Processing happens in your browser, so the image never leaves your device.
toolAbout.add-text-to-image: This tool lets you add text and emoji stickers right onto an image. Drag each element to position it, change its size and color, then download the finished picture. It's great for captions, labels, announcements, or just a bit of fun. Everything happens in your browser, so your image is never uploaded.
toolAbout.jpg-to-png: This tool converts JPG images into the PNG format. PNG supports transparency and keeps sharp edges crisp, which is ideal for logos, icons, and graphics with text. Just upload a JPG and download the PNG version. The conversion runs in your browser, so your image is never uploaded.
toolAbout.png-to-jpg: This tool converts PNG images into the JPG format. JPG files are much smaller, which makes them easier to upload, email, and share. It's a quick way to shrink heavy screenshots or graphics before sending them. The conversion happens in your browser, so your original image stays private.
toolAbout.webp-converter: This tool converts images to or from the modern WebP format. WebP keeps good quality at a much smaller size, which helps websites load faster. You can turn JPG or PNG files into WebP, or convert WebP back to a more common format. It all runs in your browser, so your images are never uploaded.
toolAbout.favicon-generator: This tool creates a favicon — the little icon shown in a browser tab — from your image. Upload a logo or picture and it produces the icon sizes a website needs. It's a quick way to give your site a polished, recognizable look. Everything is generated in your browser, so your image is never uploaded.
toolAbout.tax-calculator: This tool quickly works out tax amounts along with the before- and after-tax totals. Enter a price and rate, and it shows you the tax and the final figure at once. It's useful for invoices, quotes, shopping, or just double-checking a receipt. Calculations happen instantly in your browser and nothing you type is stored.
toolAbout.loan-calculator: This tool estimates your monthly loan payment and the total interest you'll pay. Enter the loan amount, interest rate, and term, and it lays out what each month looks like. It's a helpful way to compare options before borrowing or to plan a budget. Everything is calculated in your browser, and none of your numbers are saved.
toolAbout.mortgage-calculator: This tool estimates your monthly home-loan payment based on the price, down payment, rate, and term. It shows how much you'd pay each month and how the interest adds up over the years. It's a great first step when planning to buy a home or comparing mortgage offers. Calculations run in your browser and your figures are never stored.
toolAbout.compound-interest-calculator: This tool shows how your money can grow with compound interest over time. Enter a starting amount, rate, and period, and it projects how the balance builds as interest earns more interest. It's a clear way to picture the power of long-term saving or investing. Everything is calculated in your browser, with nothing saved.
toolAbout.percentage-calculator: This tool handles everyday percentage questions in one place. Work out what percent one number is of another, find a percentage of a value, or see the percent change between two figures. It's handy for discounts, tips, grades, statistics, and more. Calculations are instant and run entirely in your browser.
toolAbout.discount-calculator: This tool figures out the final price after a discount. Enter the original price and the percent off, and it shows both how much you save and what you'll actually pay. It's perfect for sales, coupons, and quick shopping decisions. Everything is calculated in your browser, with nothing stored.
toolAbout.tip-calculator: This tool works out the tip and the total bill in seconds. Enter the amount, choose a tip percentage, and even split it between people. It takes the guesswork out of dining out or sharing a bill with friends. Calculations are instant and stay entirely in your browser.
toolAbout.vat-calculator: This tool adds or removes VAT (value-added tax) from a price. Enter an amount and rate to see the net price, the tax, and the gross total clearly separated. It's useful for businesses, freelancers, and anyone handling invoices. Everything runs instantly in your browser and nothing is saved.
toolAbout.currency-converter: This tool converts an amount from one currency into another. Pick the currencies and enter a value to see the converted figure right away. It's handy for travel, online shopping, or keeping an eye on exchange rates. The conversion runs in your browser for a fast, fuss-free result.
toolAbout.calorie-calculator: This tool estimates how many calories your body needs each day. Based on your age, sex, height, weight, and activity level, it calculates your daily energy needs (TDEE). It's a solid starting point for losing, maintaining, or gaining weight in a planned way. Your details aren't stored and everything is calculated in your browser.
toolAbout.body-fat-calculator: This tool estimates your body fat percentage from a few simple measurements. It gives you a clearer picture of your body composition than weight alone. It's useful for tracking fitness progress over time instead of just watching the scale. Your measurements aren't stored and the result is calculated right in your browser.
toolAbout.due-date-calculator: This tool estimates a pregnancy due date from the last menstrual period. It shows the expected delivery date and how far along the pregnancy is. It's a reassuring way to plan ahead and follow the weeks. The dates you enter aren't stored and everything is calculated in your browser.
toolAbout.ideal-weight-calculator: This tool estimates a healthy weight range for your height and build. It gives you a sensible target to aim for rather than a single rigid number. It's a helpful reference when setting realistic health or fitness goals. Your details aren't stored and the result is worked out in your browser.
toolAbout.water-intake-calculator: This tool estimates how much water you should drink each day. Based on your weight and activity, it suggests a daily target to help you stay hydrated. It's a simple nudge toward a healthier routine. Your details aren't stored and the calculation happens in your browser.
toolAbout.sleep-calculator: This tool suggests the best times to go to bed or wake up based on sleep cycles. Tell it when you need to wake up, and it works back through full cycles so you rise feeling refreshed. It's a small change that can make mornings noticeably easier. Everything is calculated in your browser, with nothing stored.
toolAbout.character-counter: This tool counts the characters, words, and sentences in your text as you type. It's especially reassuring when you're writing something with a length limit, like an essay, a bio, or a social post. Everything is processed in your browser, so your text stays private.
toolAbout.string-length-counter: This tool counts the characters, words, and sentences in your text as you type. It's especially reassuring when you're writing something with a length limit, like an essay, a bio, or a social post. Everything is processed in your browser, so your text stays private.

toolAbout.encoding-converter: Open a text or CSV file saved in a legacy encoding (like Korean EUC-KR/CP949 or Japanese Shift_JIS) and convert it to UTF-8 — or the other way around. toolAbout.csv-to-excel: Drop a CSV file to get a proper Excel .xlsx workbook, or drop an .xlsx/.xls file to export it back to CSV. Conversion direction is detected from the file you drop. The CSV export includes a UTF-8 BOM so Korean and other non-ASCII text open cleanly in Excel. Handy for spreadsheets, data exports and re-importing. Everything runs in your browser — files are never uploaded — and it is free with no install.

The captured image appears as a preview.
Download it as PNG/JPG or copy it to the clipboard to paste anywhere.

toolui.pt_note: Atomic masses are standard atomic weights; for elements with no stable isotope, the mass number of the most stable isotope is shown.

toolui.mm_error: That formula isn't valid. Check element symbols (case-sensitive), numbers, and parentheses.
toolui.mm_note: Atomic masses are standard atomic weights. Parentheses and hydrate dots (·) are supported (e.g. CuSO4·5H2O).

toolui.eq_note: Range is by atomic number (1–20 basics, 1–118 all).
toolui.rc_subtitle: Pick the color bands to get the resistance value and tolerance.
toolui.rc_note: Supports 4- and 5-band resistors. Pick the colors to see the resistance (Ω/kΩ/MΩ) and tolerance.
toolui.ol_subtitle: Enter any two of voltage, current, resistance, and power to get the rest.
toolui.sn_error: Couldn't read that number. Enter it like 1500000 or 1.5e6.
toolui.bt_subtitle: Work out the blood types a child can have from the parents' blood types.
toolui.bt_note: Based on the ABO blood group system. Actual probabilities depend on the parents' genotypes (AA/AO, etc.); this shows only which blood types are possible.
toolui.pw_subtitle: Enter your weight on Earth to see what you'd weigh on other planets and the Moon.
toolui.pw_note: Based on each body's surface gravity (Earth = 1). Weight scales with gravity; your mass doesn't change.
toolui.pa_subtitle: Enter your birthdate to see your age measured in each planet's years.
toolui.pa_error: Enter a date in the past.
toolui.pa_note: Your age converted to each planet's orbital period (year length). E.g. a year on Mars is about 687 days.
toolui.mp_subtitle: Enter a date to see the shape of the Moon (its phase) on that day.

Everything is processed in your browser — your files are never uploaded. (Password-protected ZIPs aren’t supported.)
toolui.ma_subtitle: Enter your birthdate to get your age in full years instantly.
toolui.ma_error: Enter a date in the past.
toolui.ma_note: In Korea, legal and official ages were standardized to 'man-nai' (full international age) in June 2023. Counting age = this year − birth year + 1; year age = this year − birth year.
toolui.dd_subtitle: Count the days until (or since) a target date.
toolui.dd_note: Calculated from today. D-DAY means the target date itself.
toolui.wc_subtitle: Count words, characters, sentences, and get detailed text statistics instantly
toolui.wc_note: Updates in real time based on your device clock.
toolui.al_subtitle: Set a time and it will alert you with a sound.

toolui.me_subtitle: Hear a precise beat at any tempo (BPM).
toolui.me_note: All sound is generated in your browser. The accented first beat (red) helps you count the measure.
toolui.rr_subtitle: Measures frames per second to estimate your monitor's refresh rate.
toolui.rr_note: Browsers usually render in sync with the monitor's refresh rate, so the measured FPS is close to the refresh rate. Heavy background activity can lower the reading.
toolui.st_subtitle: Fill the screen with solid colors to check for dead pixels and burn-in.
toolui.st_note: Tap a color to fill the screen. Click (tap) to cycle to the next color; press Esc or ✕ to exit. A dot that never changes color is a dead pixel.
toolui.st_hint: Click for next color · Esc to exit
toolui.sp_subtitle: Play sound on the left, right, or both channels to verify them.
toolui.sp_note: Put on your earphones or headphones. If sound comes from the side you pressed, it's working.
toolui.mt_subtitle: Shows your microphone input level as a bar.
toolui.mt_denied: Microphone permission was denied. Allow it from the permission settings in your browser's address bar.
toolui.mt_note: Audio never leaves your device — it's used only for the level meter and is never recorded or saved.
toolui.kb_subtitle: Pressed keys light up, so you can find keys that don't respond (stuck or dead).
toolui.kb_note: Press each key. Keys you've pressed turn green, making it easy to spot unresponsive ones. (Some shortcuts may be intercepted by the browser.)
toolui.mo_subtitle: Check buttons, double-click, scroll, and polling rate.
toolui.mo_pad: Move or click inside this area
toolui.mo_note: Polling rate is how often the mouse reports its position (Hz). Move quickly inside the area; gaming mice reach 500–1000Hz. Browser limits may make it read lower than the real value.
toolui.av_subtitle: Enter a start date to see how many days it's been, plus 100-day and anniversary dates.
toolui.av_note: The start date counts as day 1 (Korean style); e.g. day 100 is 99 days after the start.
toolui.cz_subtitle: Encrypt or decrypt text with the classic Caesar cipher shift.
toolui.cz_note: Based on the Gregorian year. Births before Lunar New Year may belong to the previous animal.
toolui.wn_subtitle: Enter a date to see which week of the year it falls in.
toolui.wn_note: Based on ISO 8601 (weeks start Monday; week 1 contains the first Thursday).
